summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a tight ’79 show highlighted by a smoking China>Rider, Terrapin Station and a magical NFA intro.
review: The Charlie Miller SBD source delivers crystal‑clear, tight mix with no noticeable flaws, letting the band’s high energy shine from the opening ETLU through to the encore. Reviewers rave about Brent's funky keyboards, Phil’s commanding bass, Jerry’s soaring slide work and a string of standout moments – China>Rider, ShakeDown>Samson, Terrapin Station, Althea, Ship of Fools and an unforgettable NFA intro.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both performance (standout songs, tight playing) and recording quality (crisp soundboard), with no negative comments to offset the rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, tight mix; no sloppy jams or drop‑outs. Reviewers note it as the better of two Miller sources and praise the SBD patch in Jack Straw.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Show described as tight from start to finish, with energy building especially after Althea and maintaining momentum through a seamless second set.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His slide work is called "super stylie" and his high‑note leads are highlighted as spectacular.
band_member_comments.Phil: Praised for being "on and in charge"; several reviewers call the bass performance "phil‑tastic".
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent receives abundant praise – described as "the man", delivering vintage funk and key parts that drive the show.
